title: Compare volumes with unit cubes
description: Recognize volume as an attribute of solid figures and understand concepts of volume measurement.

assessment_items.question_content:
These prisms are made from the same size unit cubes.

**Which rectangular prism has greater volume?**  
*Some of the cubes might be hidden behind other cubes. Try to visualize all of the cubes.*
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can see that there are $8$ cubes on each layer of the $\maroonD{\text{first prism}}$.  There are $4$ layers, so we can find the total number of cubes by multiplying.
  
$8\times 4= \maroonD{32}$   unit cubes

[[☃ image 1]]

We can also think of the volume formula.
  
$V = l\times w\times h$  
$V = 4\times 2\times 4= \maroonD{32}$   unit cubes

We can see that there are $12$ cubes on each layer of the $\blueD{\text{second prism}}$.
There are $3$ layers, so we can find the total number of cubes by multiplying.
  
$12\times 3= \blueD{36}$   unit cubes

[[☃ image 1]]

We can also solve using the volume formula.
  
$V = l\times w\times h$  
$V = 4\times 3\times 3= \blueD{36}$   unit cubes

The $\blueD{\text{second rectangular prism}}$ has greater volume.  

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: The $2$ prisms have equal volume.

![The front face of the rectangular prism has 16 cubes: 4 rows, 4 columns. The top face of the rectangular prism has 8 cubes: 2 rows, 4 columns. The right-hand side face of the rectangular prism has 8 cubes: 4 rows, 2 columns.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2c191d4f0b8678c1d079267de19c561efc55b5b1)

![The front face of the rectangular prism has 12 cubes: 3 rows, 4 columns. The top face of the rectangular prism has 12 cubes: 3 rows, 4 columns. The right-hand side face of the rectangular prism has 9 cubes: 3 rows, 3 columns.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c89c1607346550dd1976141daa9212f809d8df63)

assessment_items.question_content:
These prisms are made from the same size unit cubes.

**Which rectangular prism has greater volume?**  
*Some of the cubes might be hidden behind other cubes. Try to visualize all of the cubes.*
[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can see that there are $6$ cubes on each layer of the $\maroonD{\text{first prism}}$.  There are $5$ layers, so we can find the total number of cubes by multiplying.
  
$6\times 5= \maroonD{30}$   unit cubes


[[☃ image 1]]

We can also think of the volume formula.
  
$V = l\times w\times h$  
$V = 2\times 3\times 5= \maroonD{30}$  unit cubes 
We can see that there are $6$ cubes on each layer of the $\blueD{\text{second prism}}$.
There are $4$ layers, so we can find the total number of cubes by multiplying.
  
$6\times 4= \blueD{24}$   unit cubes

[[☃ image 1]]

We can also solve using the volume formula.
  
$V = l\times w\times h$  
$V = 2\times 3\times 4= \blueD{24}$   unit cubes

The $\maroonD{\text{first rectangular prism}}$ has greater volume.  

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: The $2$ prisms have equal volume.

![The front face of the rectangular prism has 10 cubes: 5 rows, 2 columns. The top face of the rectangular prism has 6 cubes: 3 rows, 2 columns. The right-hand side face of the rectangular prism has 15 cubes: 5 rows, 3 columns.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ccbe73c1c8739703f212f687295f5bb0f37380f6)

![The front face of the rectangular prism has 8 cubes: 4 rows, 2 columns. The top face of the rectangular prism has 6 cubes: 3 rows, 2 columns. The right-hand side face of the rectangular prism has 12 cubes: 4 rows, 3 columns.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/481236e46b362a9a7a299aa459e6c4eebc040d72)
